title: Java Parallel Stream
description: Reduce a numeric range concurrently with a parallel stream.
learningGoals: Process a stream pipeline across multiple threads with parallel().
Recall that parallel pipelines need stateless operations and associative reductions.

completionChecks.prompt: Why is sum() a safe reduction for this parallel stream?
choices: Addition is associative and the operation is stateless
mapToLong prevents every possible race condition
completionChecks.explanation: Associative, stateless reductions can be split and combined safely across parallel stream workers.

choices: Every element runs on a new Thread object
The pipeline may execute in parallel
completionChecks.explanation: parallel() requests parallel execution, but it does not guarantee speedup or a new thread per element.

choices: The result is nondeterministic because the worker threads race
completionChecks.explanation: The trap is treating parallel as unordered math; the even numbers 2 through 1000 sum to 250500 and sum combines them safely.
